claim_boundary: A workload may be called production-ready only after every required evidence key is passed for that workload and every out-of-scope path in the same family has deterministic unsupported diagnostics. This matrix currently records blocked local v1 candidates and does not authorize production readiness.
fallback_boundary: Production certification evidence cannot come from Spark, DataFusion, DuckDB, Polars, pandas, Dask, Ray, Velox, Trino, or another external query/runtime engine executing ShardLoom work.

workloads.data_scale: bounded local files and generated-source fixtures; no production scale claim
workloads.unsupported_edge_boundary: Object-store, lakehouse/table, distributed, live/hybrid, arbitrary plugin/effect, and real Foundry execution remain outside this workload until their own production items close.

pulseweave.reason: Local single-process batch routes do not yet need scheduler-level production control, but repeated prepared-state and source work should be reviewed before certification.

capillary_work_units.reason: Local source, preparation, and output work already use bounded stage/evidence units in release and benchmark reports; production certification still needs fault and memory evidence.

dynamic_admission_work_shaping.reason: Current front-door routes admit scoped local inputs and block unsupported production families; broader shape-based production admission is not complete.

metadata_first_execution.reason: Local Vortex and prepared-state reports include metadata/source fingerprint evidence, but production certification still needs scale and failure-mode proof.

timing_surface_separation.reason: Benchmark artifacts separate hot_runtime from proof/publication timing surfaces.

evidence_tier_controls.reason: Benchmark and release evidence distinguish compact runtime evidence from publication-proof evidence.

workloads.data_scale: bounded local-emulator object fixtures and public no-credential fixture URI shapes; no live cloud, table, distributed, or scale claim
workloads.unsupported_edge_boundary: Live S3, GCS, ADLS, credential resolution, provider probing, remote cache writes, multipart cloud upload, table commits, distributed execution, and object-store Vortex Source/Sink integration remain blocked until separate evidence and maintainer-approved backend profiles close.

pulseweave.reason: The scoped local-emulator profile is single-process and bounded; PulseWeave should be applied when range coalescing, retries, backpressure, or multipart work introduces concurrent capillary tasks.

capillary_work_units.reason: Read, write, recovery, partition discovery, and commit-manifest replay are separate bounded object-store work units with explicit evidence fields.

dynamic_admission_work_shaping.reason: The runtime admits only local-emulator and public no-credential fixture profiles; live providers fail before credential resolution or network/provider probes.

metadata_first_execution.reason: The profile captures object size, modified time, URI shape, source fingerprints, sidecar commit manifests, checksums, and partition directory metadata before data claims.

timing_surface_separation.reason: The object-store profile is currently correctness and safety evidence; no performance route or timing claim is made.

evidence_tier_controls.reason: Local-emulator and public-fixture evidence is recorded as fixture-smoke only while production cloud evidence remains blocked.

evidence_refs: object-store benchmark profile not yet generated

production_blockers: live provider credential policy remains blocked
object-store Vortex Source/Sink integration remains blocked

workloads.workload_name: Local manifest table runtime v1 candidate
workloads.data_scale: bounded in-memory local manifest metadata plus local committed manifest and sidecar files; no object-store, catalog, external table protocol, production scale, or lakehouse claim
workloads.unsupported_edge_boundary: Iceberg, Delta, Hudi, external catalogs, object-store table commits, merge/update/delete, production lakehouse transactions, distributed execution, and production performance claims remain blocked until separate source-checked protocol and runtime evidence closes.

pulseweave.reason: The scoped local-manifest profile is single-process and fixture-bounded; PulseWeave should apply when snapshot planning, object-store manifests, retries, or multi-split commits introduce concurrent capillary tasks.

capillary_work_units.reason: Metadata read, append commit rehearsal, sidecar commit record, rollback cleanup, and recovery replay are separate bounded table work units with explicit evidence fields.

dynamic_admission_work_shaping.reason: Only the ShardLoom-owned local-manifest fixture profile is admitted; external table formats, catalogs, object stores, and mutation semantics fail before external effects.

metadata_first_execution.reason: The profile captures schema, snapshot, manifest, segment, row-count, digest, idempotency, and sidecar recovery evidence before any table claim.

timing_surface_separation.reason: The local-manifest table profile is correctness and safety evidence; no table performance route or timing claim is made.

evidence_tier_controls.reason: Local-manifest table evidence is fixture-smoke only while production lakehouse/table-format evidence remains blocked.

evidence_refs: table benchmark profile not yet generated

production_blockers: external Iceberg/Delta/Hudi source-checked protocol proof missing
external catalog and object-store table commit semantics remain blocked
production lakehouse transaction claim remains blocked

workloads.workload_name: Local distributed fixture runtime v1 candidate
workloads.data_scale: bounded three-split in-memory fixture with two default local worker slots and scoped local hash repartition/local combine/global merge evidence; no remote worker, object-store, remote shuffle, spill IO, production scale, or performance claim
workloads.unsupported_edge_boundary: Remote worker services, network coordinators, object-store split distribution, remote shuffle/repartition, distributed spill/backpressure, multi-host execution, and distributed performance claims remain blocked until separate production evidence exists.

pulseweave.reason: The local fixture records a coordinator-worker attempt graph with leases, heartbeats, retry, duplicate-attempt rejection, stale-lease rejection, scoped local hash repartition, skew evidence, backpressure accounting, and deterministic merge evidence.

capillary_work_units.reason: The split manifest uses bounded split units with per-split worker assignment, attempt refs, result fragments, and merge digest evidence.

dynamic_admission_work_shaping.reason: The runtime admits only one to four local worker slots and blocks remote/distributed production semantics through explicit claim and unsupported rows.

metadata_first_execution.reason: The fixture uses manifest row-count and estimated-byte metadata to plan local split assignment, reduce partitioning, and memory budget accounting before execution.

timing_surface_separation.reason: No distributed benchmark timing surface is published yet; future distributed timing must separate hot split execution from proof/merge/reporting work.

evidence_tier_controls.reason: The command emits execution and Native I/O certificates while keeping production and performance claims disabled.

production_blockers: remote worker service and network coordinator proof missing
remote shuffle/repartition and distributed spill/backpressure remain blocked beyond scoped local fixture evidence
multi-host fault injection and cleanup evidence missing

workloads.workload_name: Live/hybrid local checkpoint fixture v1 candidate
workloads.data_scale: bounded ten-change in-memory fixture with explicit local checkpoint JSON, changelog JSONL, readback digest, and restored key-count verification; no broker, object-store checkpoint store, Vortex micro-segment persistence, cold promotion, exactly-once, production scale, or performance claim
workloads.unsupported_edge_boundary: Broker adapters, unbounded schedulers, production durable state stores, object-store/catalog checkpoints, Vortex micro-segment persistence, cold Vortex segment promotion, deletion-vector persistence, exactly-once semantics, and benchmark performance claims remain blocked until separate production evidence exists.

pulseweave.reason: The bounded fixture does not need runtime control loops yet; production live/hybrid scheduling must apply PulseWeave control before making freshness or throughput claims.

capillary_work_units.reason: The checkpoint proof uses a small capillary state unit: fixture changes, active state, checkpoint payload, changelog payload, readback, digest, and certificate evidence.

dynamic_admission_work_shaping.reason: The command admits only caller-provided local filesystem directories and rejects remote URI-like paths before writing.

metadata_first_execution.reason: The fixture records deterministic record counts, key counts, checkpoint policy, restore status, and digests before allowing the smoke to pass.

timing_surface_separation.reason: No live/hybrid benchmark timing surface is published yet; future timing must separate hot update runtime from checkpoint/proof work.

evidence_tier_controls.reason: The command emits freshness, state, execution, and Native I/O evidence while keeping production and exactly-once claims disabled.

workloads.data_scale: bounded local extension manifest JSON up to 256 KiB per file, approved local manifest directories up to 64 JSON manifests / 2 MiB aggregate, and in-process typed UDF registry metadata rows; no dependency expansion, arbitrary UDF runtime, plugin runtime, production scale, or performance claim
workloads.unsupported_edge_boundary: Arbitrary UDF/plugin runtime, dynamic library loading, WASM/Python execution, dependency expansion, filesystem/network/secret effects, external API/LLM/model/vector operations, ambient plugin-path scanning, and production extension platform claims remain blocked until separate safety and runtime evidence closes.

pulseweave.reason: Manifest inspection and approved directory discovery are bounded metadata paths. PulseWeave should be reconsidered only when registry scans become parallel or when policy-admitted effect batches exist; it must not hide effect or materialization boundaries.

capillary_work_units.reason: Local path admission, bounded read, JSON parse, manifest-contract validation, directory inventory, duplicate-ID detection, review classification, and no-effect evidence are separated as deterministic inspection units.

dynamic_admission_work_shaping.reason: Only explicit local manifest files or approved local manifest directories with the current schema are admitted; remote URIs, symlinks, oversized files/directories, unsupported schema versions, duplicate IDs, runtime execution, and effectful support claims fail closed.

metadata_first_execution.reason: The workload inspects declared manifest metadata only and emits capability, permission, effect, sandbox, license, provenance, ABI, lifecycle, runtime, determinism, materialization, null, dtype, timeout, memory, CPU, retry, idempotency, audit, and review fields before any runtime can be enabled.

timing_surface_separation.reason: Manifest inspection is safety and release evidence; no benchmark route or performance timing claim is made.

evidence_tier_controls.reason: Metadata inspection evidence is distinct from any future runtime extension, UDF, external effect, or production platform claim.

evidence_refs: approved local manifest directory discovery implemented
oversized file admission blocked by bounded read
manifest inspection has no benchmark route
extension runtime and registry scan performance profiles not generated

production_blockers: metadata-only manifest registry is not arbitrary plugin runtime support
typed UDF registry is metadata-only beyond the admitted built-in scalar fixture
arbitrary UDF runtime execution bridge remains blocked
sandbox enforcement for dynamic plugin/runtime execution remains blocked
